official_functions.role: Construct the official Play clientbound pong_response fixture
Write, decode, and observe the official pong_response body
Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns pong_response to packet id 0x3e.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the pong_response id to ClientboundPongResponsePacket.
The official pong_response body is one big-endian long time.
The fixture uses a primitive constructor value and does not require initialized client/server state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded time, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte counts are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove runtime ping/pong latency behavior, Play entry, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer runtime ping/pong latency behavior, Play entry, render readiness, or client-load completion.
